zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Beauchamp於1396年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼時，她的父親William de Beauchamp53歲，母親Joan FitzAlan29歲。她於1413年8月28日與James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond在英國格盧斯特斯爾金也斯 斯坦利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1430年8月5日在英國素瑞里士滿享年34歲，埋葬在英國倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Beauchamp于1396年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼时，她的父亲William de Beauchamp53岁，母亲Joan FitzAlan29岁。她于1413年8月28日与James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond在英国格盧斯特斯爾金也斯 斯坦利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1430年8月5日在英国素瑞里士滿享年34岁，埋葬在英国倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Beauchamp was born in 1396, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, her father, William de Beauchamp, was 53 and her mother, Joan FitzAlan, was 29. She married James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond on 28 August 1413, in King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 5 August 1430, in Richmond, Surrey, England, at the age of 34, and was buried in London, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Beauchamp est née en 1396, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Joan FitzAlan, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond le 28 août 1413, à King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 août 1430, à Richmond, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Beauchamp 1396 in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Beauchamp 53 und ihre Mutter Joan FitzAlan 29. Sie hat am 28. August 1413 in King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, England James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. August 1430 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Richmond, Surrey, England und wurde in London, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1396 nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, sua padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 53 anni e sua madre, Joan FitzAlan, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond il 28 agosto 1413, nel King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 5 agosto 1430, nel Richmond, Surrey, England, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a London, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Beauchampが1396年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは53歳，母Joan FitzAlanは29歳であった。1413年8月28日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアキング'ス スタンリーでJames Butler 4th Earl of Ormondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1430年8月5日にイギリススリーリチモンドで34歳で死去し，イギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Beauchamp(이)가 1396년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 53세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan FitzAlan(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1413년 8월 28일 영국 그루스스터실르 킨그'스 스탠리에서 James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1430년 8월 5일 영국 수리 리ch몽에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Beauchamp nasceu em 1396, em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Joan FitzAlan, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond em 28 de agosto de 1413, em King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 5 de agosto de 1430, em Richmond, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em London, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Beauchamp родилась в 1396 г. в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Joan FitzAlan, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond 28 августа 1413 г. в местности кинг'с стэнли, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 5 августа 1430 г. в местности ричмонд, сурри, Англия в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Beauchamp nació en 1396, en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Joan FitzAlan, tenía 29 años. Se casó con James Butler 4th Earl of Ormond el 28 de agosto de 1413, en King's Stanley, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 5 de agosto de 1430, en Richmond, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en London, Inglaterra.
